A lot of folks don't have a one-step sales process. Like, if I'm selling a jet, I'm probably not making a one-call close on that. So what happens... I wouldn't rule it out, but you're right more often than not. So what happens when you have to make to sell in a multiple-step sales process? Well, I think the first thing you do is you don't make the mistake that our friend did earlier and not having all the decision makers in the room when you're putting that together. So that's step number one. So anybody that... The final decision makers need to be in the room. So once you have that, it's... You're gonna have to... I use the covered wagon. You need to circle them. Make sure that nobody else can penetrate that circle. And that's through marketing. That's through shock in all boxes. You're gonna do some social media searches to make sure that you have the understanding of what their hot buttons are. In realizing at different levels of the organization, it's pain and pleasure. So some people are gonna not make decisions just because they're afraid or they don't want to lose their job or be a fear of being wrong. So you need to take that off the table for them. And there's others that... If they're the CEO, he wants to drive the top line or he wants to improve the bottom line. And so you need to talk to that language. You need to understand all the players. Who are your angels within the organization? What's their motivation? You need to find a rabbi, somebody to kind of carry you through the organization. But you really don't make the mistake that our friend did in the second segment of not having all the decision makers in the room every time you're doing a presentation. Yeah. And look, you can... I mean, when I switched to flying private, I already knew. Right, you just wanted to buy it. I just want to buy it. See, the worst thing is, Matt's a Google, calls it the Google slap, is you go to all the trouble of the marketing, the education, the affluent person is now sat through your workshop or come and met with you, and now he goes home in Google's annuities. You're like, oh no. So you've got to have a preemptive process to stop him from doing that. Right? In some cases, that's, if you are going to go shop this, here's the shopping guide.
